# Review: `deepmind/935.lean` ÔÇö Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
- [ ] **FAIL:** Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem. The module docstring contains only the heading and reference link ÔÇö no description of what the problem is about (powerful parts of consecutive products, bounds on QÔéé, etc.). The individual theorem docstrings describe each part, but the module-level docstring itself lacks a summary.
- N/A: Optional bibliography entries (not present, acceptable).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos935 ... end Erdos935`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos935`).
- [x] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block (`open Finset` and `open scoped BigOperators Real` on lines 25ÔÇô27, before `namespace` on line 29).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `powerfulPart` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Marked `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Function returning a type uses `lowerCamelCase` (`powerfulPart`).
- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional; "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All three declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_935` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] All names follow `snake_case` conventions (`erdos_935`, `erdos_935.variants.part2`, `erdos_935.variants.part3`).
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line.
- [x] Main theorem and all variants have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings describe the mathematical content. (No short-form paper citations like `[Er95]`, but the descriptions are informative; soft requirement.)

### `answer()` usage

**`erdos_935` (Part 1)** ÔÇö Docstring: "Is it true that ÔÇŽ" Ôćĺ yes/no question Ôćĺ Case 1:
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) Ôćö P`. Correct.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked.
- [x] Quantifiers (`ÔłÇ Ôäô`, `ÔłÇ ╬Á`, `Ôłâ nÔéÇ`, `ÔłÇ n`) are on the RHS of `Ôćö`, not as theorem parameters.

**`erdos_935.variants.part2`** ÔÇö Docstring: states a proved fact ("For every Ôäô Ôëą 2, limsup ÔÇŽ = Ôł×") Ôćĺ direct assertion Ôćĺ Case 3:
- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly, no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for a solved result stated as an assertion.

**`erdos_935.variants.part3`** ÔÇö Docstring: "Is it true that ÔÇŽ" Ôćĺ yes/no question Ôćĺ Case 1:
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) Ôćö P`. Correct.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked.
- [x] Quantifiers are on the RHS of `Ôćö`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values are valid: `research open` (erdos_935, part3), `research solved` (part2).
- [x] `research open` theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed (`11`).
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (number theory).
- N/A: Only one number per theorem, so ordering and duplicates are not applicable.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjectures use `by sorry` (multi-line `by\n  sorry` format, acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. (Part 2's docstring mentions the Pell equation proof method, but Part 2 is `research solved`, so this is appropriate.)
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_935.variants.part2` and `erdos_935.variants.part3`.
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variants have docstrings describing what they formalize.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `ÔëĄ`, `Ôłł`, `ÔäĽ`, `ÔäŁ`, `Ôćö`, `Ôćĺ`, `╬Á`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified.

---

## Summary

**Result: 1 issue found.**

| # | Severity | Section | Issue |
|---|----------|---------|-------|
| 1 | Minor | ┬ž3 Module Docstring | Missing plain-English description of the problem in the module docstring. Add a brief summary (e.g., the problem concerns bounding the powerful part QÔéé of products of consecutive integers). |

The formalization is otherwise clean and well-structured. The `answer()` usage is correct across all three parts, the Case 1/3 distinctions are properly applied, the custom definition is well-documented and marked `noncomputable`, and the variant naming follows conventions.

    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ/-!
    20Ôćĺ# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/935](https://www.erdosproblems.com/935)
    23Ôćĺ-/
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺopen Finset
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺopen scoped BigOperators Real
    28Ôćĺ
    29Ôćĺnamespace Erdos935
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ/-- The powerful (2-full) part of a natural number $n$: the product of all prime
    32Ôćĺpower factors $p^a$ where $a \geq 2$. -/
    33Ôćĺnoncomputable def powerfulPart (n : ÔäĽ) : ÔäĽ :=
    34Ôćĺ  (n.factorization.support.filter (fun p => 2 ÔëĄ n.factorization p)).prod
    35Ôćĺ    (fun p => p ^ n.factorization p)
    36Ôćĺ
    37Ôćĺ/--
    38ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 1:
    39Ôćĺ
    40ÔćĺIs it true that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\ell \geq 1$, if $n$ is sufficiently large then
    41Ôćĺ$$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < n^{2+\varepsilon}?$$
    42Ôćĺ-/
    43Ôćĺ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    44Ôćĺtheorem erdos_935 :
    45Ôćĺ    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    46Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 1 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    47Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
    49Ôćĺ      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
    50Ôćĺ        (n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((2 : ÔäŁ) + ╬Á) := by
    51Ôćĺ  sorry
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ/--
    54ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 2 (resolved):
    55Ôćĺ
    56ÔćĺFor every $\ell \geq 2$, $\limsup_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^2 = \infty$.
    57ÔćĺThis was proved via solutions to the Pell equation $x^2 - 8y^2 = 1$.
    58ÔćĺFormulated as: for every $M$ and $N$, there exists $n \geq N$ such that
    59Ôćĺ$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) > M \cdot n^2$.
    60Ôćĺ-/
    61Ôćĺ@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    62Ôćĺtheorem erdos_935.variants.part2 :
    63Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    64Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ M : ÔäŁ, ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ,
    65Ôćĺ    Ôłâ n : ÔäĽ, N ÔëĄ n Ôłž
    66Ôćĺ      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) >
    67Ôćĺ        M * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ (2 : ÔäĽ)) := by
    68Ôćĺ  sorry
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ/--
    71ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 3:
    72Ôćĺ
    73ÔćĺIs it true that for every $\ell \geq 2$,
    74Ôćĺ$\lim_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^{\ell+1} = 0$?
    75ÔćĺFormulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$,
    76Ôćĺ$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < \varepsilon \cdot n^{\ell+1}$ for all sufficiently large $n$.
    77Ôćĺ-/
    78Ôćĺ@[category research open, AMS 11]
    79Ôćĺtheorem erdos_935.variants.part3 :
    80Ôćĺ    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    81Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    82Ôćĺ    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
    85Ôćĺ        ╬Á * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((Ôäô : ÔäŁ) + 1)) := by
    86Ôćĺ  sorry
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺend Erdos935
    89Ôćĺ

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/935](https://www.erdosproblems.com/935)
-/

open Finset

open scoped BigOperators Real

namespace Erdos935

/-- The powerful (2-full) part of a natural number $n$: the product of all prime
power factors $p^a$ where $a \geq 2$. -/
noncomputable def powerfulPart (n : ÔäĽ) : ÔäĽ :=
  (n.factorization.support.filter (fun p => 2 ÔëĄ n.factorization p)).prod
    (fun p => p ^ n.factorization p)

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 1:

Is it true that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\ell \geq 1$, if $n$ is sufficiently large then
$$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < n^{2+\varepsilon}?$$
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935 :
    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 1 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
        (n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((2 : ÔäŁ) + ╬Á) := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 2 (resolved):

For every $\ell \geq 2$, $\limsup_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^2 = \infty$.
This was proved via solutions to the Pell equation $x^2 - 8y^2 = 1$.
Formulated as: for every $M$ and $N$, there exists $n \geq N$ such that
$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) > M \cdot n^2$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935.variants.part2 :
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ M : ÔäŁ, ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ,
    Ôłâ n : ÔäĽ, N ÔëĄ n Ôłž
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) >
        M * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ (2 : ÔäĽ)) := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 3:

Is it true that for every $\ell \geq 2$,
$\lim_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^{\ell+1} = 0$?
Formulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$,
$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < \varepsilon \cdot n^{\ell+1}$ for all sufficiently large $n$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935.variants.part3 :
    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
        ╬Á * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((Ôäô : ÔäŁ) + 1)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos935

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ┬ž1ÔÇô7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [ ] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  ```
  /-
  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
  you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
  You may obtain a copy of the License at

      https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
  limitations under the License.
  -/
  ```
- [ ] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).

## 2. Import Statement

- [ ] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  ```
  import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
  ```
- [ ] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [ ] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [ ] Starts with a heading: `# Erdos Problem N` (or appropriate title).
- [ ] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source, e.g.:
  ```
  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/N](https://www.erdosproblems.com/N)
  ```
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [ ] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.

## 4. Namespace

- [ ] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace ErdosN ... end ErdosN`).
  - Linter `linter.style.namespace` enforces this; error: *"The declaration '...' is not inside a namespace."*
- [ ] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `Erdos42`, not `erdos_42`).
- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

content.text: Clear. The module docstring needs a brief plain-English description. I'll add one based on the theorem docstrings.

input.new_string:
/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935

This problem concerns the powerful (2-full) part $Q_2$ of products of consecutive integers
$n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)$. It asks whether $Q_2 < n^{2+\varepsilon}$ for all sufficiently large $n$,
and whether $Q_2 / n^{\ell+1} \to 0$ as $n \to \infty$.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/935](https://www.erdosproblems.com/935)
-/

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935

This problem concerns the powerful (2-full) part $Q_2$ of products of consecutive integers
$n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)$. It asks whether $Q_2 < n^{2+\varepsilon}$ for all sufficiently large $n$,
and whether $Q_2 / n^{\ell+1} \to 0$ as $n \to \infty$.

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/935](https://www.erdosproblems.com/935)
-/
toolUseResult.originalFile:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/935](https://www.erdosproblems.com/935)
-/

open Finset

open scoped BigOperators Real

namespace Erdos935

/-- The powerful (2-full) part of a natural number $n$: the product of all prime
power factors $p^a$ where $a \geq 2$. -/
noncomputable def powerfulPart (n : ÔäĽ) : ÔäĽ :=
  (n.factorization.support.filter (fun p => 2 ÔëĄ n.factorization p)).prod
    (fun p => p ^ n.factorization p)

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 1:

Is it true that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\ell \geq 1$, if $n$ is sufficiently large then
$$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < n^{2+\varepsilon}?$$
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935 :
    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 1 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
        (n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((2 : ÔäŁ) + ╬Á) := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 2 (resolved):

For every $\ell \geq 2$, $\limsup_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^2 = \infty$.
This was proved via solutions to the Pell equation $x^2 - 8y^2 = 1$.
Formulated as: for every $M$ and $N$, there exists $n \geq N$ such that
$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) > M \cdot n^2$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935.variants.part2 :
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ M : ÔäŁ, ÔłÇ N : ÔäĽ,
    Ôłâ n : ÔäĽ, N ÔëĄ n Ôłž
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) >
        M * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ (2 : ÔäĽ)) := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 935, Part 3:

Is it true that for every $\ell \geq 2$,
$\lim_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^{\ell+1} = 0$?
Formulated as: for every $\varepsilon > 0$,
$Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)) < \varepsilon \cdot n^{\ell+1}$ for all sufficiently large $n$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 11]
theorem erdos_935.variants.part3 :
    answer(sorry) Ôćö
    ÔłÇ Ôäô : ÔäĽ, 2 ÔëĄ Ôäô Ôćĺ
    ÔłÇ ╬Á : ÔäŁ, 0 < ╬Á Ôćĺ
    Ôłâ nÔéÇ : ÔäĽ, ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, nÔéÇ ÔëĄ n Ôćĺ
      (powerfulPart (ÔłĆ i Ôłł Finset.range (Ôäô + 1), (n + i)) : ÔäŁ) <
        ╬Á * ((n : ÔäŁ) ^ ((Ôäô : ÔäŁ) + 1)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos935

lines: +This problem concerns the powerful (2-full) part $Q_2$ of products of consecutive integers
+$n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell)$. It asks whether $Q_2 < n^{2+\varepsilon}$ for all sufficiently large $n$,

content.text: Added a brief plain-English description of the problem to the module docstring, resolving the review's only finding.
